reasoning: The customer, Patricia Johnson, has a history of fraudulent refund requests. The system notes that she has 9 refunds in 35 days, all claiming 'item not received' despite tracking confirming delivery. Her refund rate is 82%, and the total refunded amount is $1588.19. This pattern strongly indicates abuse of the refund policy. Although the current claim is 'package was crushed in shipping,' the historical behavior warrants immediate escalation to Loss Prevention to prevent further fraudulent activity. Processing this refund would violate company policy and risk financial loss.
